target.title: fix(cli): disable ScheduleWakeup/CronCreate in --print claude runs
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `ScheduleWakeup` and `CronCreate` are Claude Code built-in tools that schedule deferred re-invocations managed by the persistent CLI runtime. In OpenClaw's one-shot `claude -p` runs the process exits at `end_turn`, so any wakeup or cron registered during the run has no host to fire into — it is silently lost. Symptom: a CLI session spawns a background sub-agent, calls `ScheduleWakeup` to poll for completion, ends the turn, and never picks up the result — work finishes unreviewed with `lastMessagePreview: NO_REPLY`.
- **Solution:** Append `--disallowedTools "ScheduleWakeup, CronCreate"` to both `args` and `resumeArgs` in the anthropic CLI backend so the model cannot invoke tools that don't survive the run mode.
- **What changed:** `extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts` — +4 lines in two static arrays.
- **What did NOT change:** Codex / Gemini backends, interactive TUI mode, Pi-backed sessions, `--allowedTools` surface, normalisation logic.

## Motivation

In a real OpenClaw deployment, an agent session running through `claude -p` called `ScheduleWakeup(270s)` to poll a background sub-agent, then ended the turn. The wakeup never fired — session showed `status: done` / `lastMessagePreview: NO_REPLY` while the sub-agent's file changes sat unreviewed on a feature branch. The right pattern for background work in a `--print` session is `Monitor` on the output file, or a synchronous sub-agent. Disabling the tools at the argv level ensures the model can't reach for an escape hatch that doesn't work in this mode.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway/orchestration

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #83552 (same class: `--print` CLI session harness gaps)
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behaviour proof

- **Behaviour addressed:** `ScheduleWakeup` called inside `claude -p` → wakeup silently dropped, session finishes with `NO_REPLY`, background sub-agent result never reviewed.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux (WSL2), Node 24, OpenClaw v2026.5.18 stable, anthropic claude-cli backend, `agent:main:*` webchat lane.
- **Observed result after fix:** `ScheduleWakeup` / `CronCreate` produces a tool-not-allowed error; model falls back to `Monitor` on the output file. The session stays open until the sub-agent completes.
- **What was not tested:** Codex / Gemini backends, Pi-backed orchestrator (Pi wakeup path is unaffected).

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `ScheduleWakeup` / `CronCreate` are unconditionally surfaced by the Claude CLI regardless of run mode. OpenClaw's one-shot `-p` invocations exit at `end_turn`; no daemon remains to service the wakeup.
- **Missing detection/guardrail:** The anthropic CLI backend `allowedTools` restricted MCP tools (`mcp__openclaw__*`) but not Claude Code built-ins.
- **Contributing context:** Pi harness has its own wakeup dispatch (correct); the CLI backend did not restrict the surface to match what the run mode can actually support.

## Regression Test Plan

N/A — the failing scenario requires a live Claude Code binary executing a real one-shot run. The change is a two-entry append to two static string arrays; manual verification is sufficient.

- [x] Existing coverage already sufficient (argv content is not unit-tested today)
- **If no new test is added, why not:** The only meaningful test is an integration test against a real `claude -p` binary, outside the scope of this minimal fix.

## User-visible / Behaviour Changes

Models running inside OpenClaw's `claude -p` sessions can no longer call `ScheduleWakeup` or `CronCreate`. They will receive a tool-not-allowed error and should fall back to `Monitor` (for background sub-agent output) or synchronous Bash with a generous timeout.

## Diagram

```text
Before:
[claude -p] -> model calls ScheduleWakeup(270s) -> process exits -> wakeup ghost, NO_REPLY

After:
[claude -p --disallowedTools ScheduleWakeup,CronCreate] -> tool-not-allowed -> model uses Monitor or sync Bash
Security Impact
New permissions/capabilities? No
Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes — reduced (two built-in tools blocked in --print mode)
All other: No
Risk: none; this is a surface reduction, not an expansion.
Repro + Verification
Environment
OS: Linux (WSL2 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2)
Runtime: Node 24.15, OpenClaw v2026.5.18
Model/provider: anthropic / claude-cli backend (claude-opus-4-7, claude-sonnet-4-6)
Channel: webchat/agent:main:* lane
Steps
Open an OpenClaw CLI-backed session (claude -p --resume <id> --allowedTools mcp__openclaw__*)
Have the model call ScheduleWakeup
Observe: before patch — silently succeeds, wakeup lost; after patch — tool-not-allowed, model recovers
Expected
Model receives tool-not-allowed and routes to Monitor or synchronous Bash.

Actual (after patch)
ScheduleWakeup and CronCreate rejected at the Claude Code built-in tool layer. Session stays coherent.

Human Verification
Verified: --disallowedTools flag accepted by claude binary; pnpm tsgo clean on patched file.
Edge cases checked: resumeArgs path (session resume) receives the same flag; normalization functions do not strip it.
Not verified: Downstream effects if a plugin explicitly re-allows these tools via its own argv injection.
Compatibility / Migration
Backwards compatible? Yes — silent success was already broken (wakeup never fired). Explicit error is strictly better.
Config/env changes? No
Migration needed? No
Risks and Mitigations
Risk: model that relied on ScheduleWakeup in a --print session gets a tool error instead of a silent success.
Mitigation: silent success was already broken. An explicit error lets the model branch to a working alternative.
target.author: skywolfdreamer
